description_long: This is a small, twin-engine turboprop passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The exterior is predominantly white with blue and red accent stripes running along the fuselage, and the tail features a distinctive geometric pattern in blue and red. The aircraft has a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels and a nose wheel visible. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, featuring multiple windows for pilot visibility. Entry is provided by a side door with an integrated stairway, and the airplane is equipped with propellers on each wing. The design suggests it is intended for short-haul regional flights, accommodating a small number of passengers.
description: A small, twin-engine turboprop passenger airplane with a white body, blue and red stripes, and a high-mounted straight wing. It features a forward cockpit, tricycle landing gear, and a side entry door with stairs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the entry door with stairs extended, propellers, and the full length of the fuselage with blue and red stripes.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail design, twin engines, and the tricycle landing gear with both main wheels visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the uninterrupted fuselage, propellers, and the geometric tail pattern.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the cockpit windows, nose, propellers, and the high-mounted straight wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed and extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
